prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Investigating the Crosslinking, Degradation, and Adhesion Be | Statement: Hot-humid aged EVA and POE coupons experienced a larger decrease in Gc compared to hot-dry aging due to enhanced hydrolytic degradation.
2. [SILVER] Source: Investigating the Crosslinking, Degradation, and Adhesion Be | Statement: FTIR-ATR analysis showed insignificant bulk encapsulant degradation, demonstrating the critical role of UV and moisture in accelerating degradation.
3. [SILVER] Source: Investigating the Crosslinking, Degradation, and Adhesion Be | Statement: Thermal degradation of the interface can be significant even when bulk encapsulant shows negligible degradation without UV and elevated humidity.
4. [SILVER] Source: Dependence of adhesion on degradation mechanisms of ethylene | Statement: EVA at the glass interface degrades more than at the cell interface, explaining why this initially stronger interface becomes preferential for delamin
5. [SILVER] Source: The Natural and Accelerated Evolution of EVA Adhesion Throug | Statement: Both field and accelerated aging reveal a distinct adhesion plateau after 1 year in field and after 1,000 hours in a chamber.
